synthesis.handoff_line: EXECUTION SKELETON (for fern #357, the 'if GO, here is the action'): the relax-prize is the int4-Marlin K-reduction split-K re-partition (+17.0..+29.3 over deployed 481.53); NO Python knob -> needs a patched-Marlin wheel BUILD (the in-tree `use_fp32_reduce=True->False` flag is only a +0.64 proxy). 10 <PENDING #452> slots. VALIDATE: (a) TPS CI-clean >= deployed+B at 1 sigma_hw [recommend clause-1]; (b) PPL <= 2.42 [clause-2]; (c) same-KIND flips [clause-3]; (d) 128/128 [precondition]. execution_verdict() = imported recommend() (GO->SHIP-READY / CI-AMBIGUOUS->HOLD / NO-GO->ROLLBACK) + 128/128 gate. ROLLBACK = single-knob byte-for-byte return to 481.53. Applying is HUMAN-GATED (Directive #3). || RESOLVED 2026-06-16: stark #452 LANDED NEGATIVE (466.20 TPS dominated, identity 0.730, cascading new-KIND; PPL 2.3782 passed). The SHIPPED execution_verdict() returns ROLLBACK (bar-invariant) — the relax lane is CLOSED. PPL alone would have waved it through; the TPS-domination + KIND clauses caught it. fern reads this as 'the gate, validated against the config that failed it.'

imports.machinery: imports #462 recommend(); round-trips committed #462/#458/#457/#448 JSONs; re-derives nothing
imports.provenance: land#462 sb1n4aa6 (recommend() IMPORTED verbatim; decision_flip_tps_threshold 12.2346, max_admissible_relax_ppl 2.42, ppl margin 0.0428, most-sensitive ppl) x land#458 uhhyec0q (gains +17.05/+29.34, gate margin 0.0428, deployed-off-strict reframe) x land#457 h0uggl9i (= ubel #450 c5oyb7gv roofline: realistic split-K 498.58, ceiling 510.87) x stark#448 fn4iz0dz (served int4-GEMM knob audit: Marlin unique on sm86, use_fp32_reduce=True default, no byte-exact selectable headroom, prize requires build, fp32off UB +0.64) x stark#452 daqrzr99/00ovtdnt (LANDED NEGATIVE: realized relax 466.20 TPS dominated, identity 0.730, 3317 cascading new-KIND flips, PPL 2.3782 passed -> gate ROLLBACK, relax lane CLOSED). The parameterized <PENDING #452> form is KEPT alongside the realized overlay. All run-ids in wandb-applied-ai-team/gemma-challenge-senpai.

prewire_stark452.execution_verdict_signature: execution_verdict(measured_tps, measured_ppl, flip_kind_same, completed, human_bar_tps, k=1)
prewire_stark452.handed_to_fern_357: fern #357 presents the one-screen GO/NO-GO + this skeleton as the 'if GO, here is the exact next action'. This card does NOT duplicate the capstone verdict — it produces the execution wrapper only.
prewire_stark452.live_status: the moment stark #452 reports (TPS, PPL, flip-kind, N, completed), each fills a slot and execution_verdict() returns the ACTION. RESOLVED 2026-06-16: stark #452 LANDED (466.20 TPS dominated, identity 0.730, cascading new-KIND) -> bar-INVARIANT ROLLBACK (see realized_stark452). The generic skeleton would resolve to SHIP-READY only on PPL <= 2.42 + same-kind + 128/128 at a human bar <= +12.23; the realized config clears NONE of {TPS, KIND}, so the gate refuses it.

prewire_stark452.wraps: #462 recommend() (imported verbatim): GO->SHIP-READY, CI-AMBIGUOUS->HOLD, NO-GO->ROLLBACK; plus the 128/128 validity precondition (< 128 -> ROLLBACK).

caught_by: clause-a (TPS dominated; gain -15.33 <= 0 < any bar; not CI-clean of any B)
clause-c (new-KIND cascading divergence; not same-family near-ties)
realized_stark452.hold_vs_rollback: the gate returns the HARD ROLLBACK, strictly stronger than HOLD. HOLD (== recommend() CI-AMBIGUOUS) is IMPOSSIBLE here: CI-AMBIGUOUS requires same_kind=True, but the realized flip-KIND is cascading divergence (same_kind=False). The config also fails clause-a (gain -15.33 <= 0, dominated). Two independent hard NO-GO clauses -> ROLLBACK. Both 'HOLD' and 'ROLLBACK' sit inside the advisor's 'never SHIP-READY' envelope; the precise machine output is ROLLBACK.
realized_stark452.interpretation: REAL-DATA VALIDATION of the execution gate: the relax split-K re-partition was built and benchmarked (stark #452) and came back DOMINATED (466.20 < deployed 481.53, gain -15.33; < strict 467.14 too) with a cascading identity collapse (0.9966 -> 0.730, 3 -> 3317 flips). The skeleton's execution_verdict() — the exact function it ships — returns ROLLBACK, refusing to ship a dominated, identity-breaking config, INVARIANT to the human bar B (even B=0). Notably PPL (the #462 most-sensitive input) PASSED (2.3782 <= 2.42): PPL alone would have waved it through. The TPS-domination + KIND clauses are what caught it — vindicating the multi-clause design and the count-vs-KIND orthogonality (3317 flips is a count; the KIND is what gates). The relax lane is CLOSED; this card is now 'the execution gate, validated against the config that tried and failed to clear it.'

realized_measurement.flip_kind: cascading free-running divergence (NOT same-family accumulation-order near-ties)

revert_mechanism.how: restore the deployed config: re-point submissions/fa2sw_precache_kenyan/manifest.json dependencies[0] back to the stock vllm-0.22.1rc1.dev307+g3e8afdf78 (manifest.dependencies[0], version-pinned) (undo knob B) and restore use_fp32_reduce=True (undo the knob-A proxy). Both are a one-line manifest/flag revert.
revert_mechanism.why_byte_for_byte: the deployed config is fully specified and version-pinned (manifest dependency + env), so reverting reproduces the deployed 481.53 config exactly — no residual state.

rollback_triggers_any_of.maps_to: validation clause-a fail (gain below bar -> NO-GO; gain clears point-only -> HOLD)
rollback_triggers_any_of.trigger: TPS gain NOT CI-clean of human bar B (gain - sigma_hw < B)

rollback_spec.summary: ROLLBACK if ANY of {PPL > 2.42; new-kind flips; TPS gain not CI-clean of B; < 128/128}. Revert is a SINGLE-KNOB, byte-for-byte return to the deployed 481.53 config (re-point the pinned wheel + restore the stock flag).

human_gated.directive: operator Directive #3 — served-file change + leaderboard submission are HUMAN-GATED actions
human_gated.required_before_apply: an `Approval request: HF job for <submission-name>` GitHub issue (PR/branch, exact command, expected metric movement / PPL risk, quota/runtime risk, local checks, artifact paths) + explicit human approval in that issue, THEN the single allowed leaderboard submission
human_gated.this_card_does: SPECIFIES the change (approval-ready); does NOT make it. analysis_only=true, no_served_file_change=true.

knob_A_in_tree_python_proxy.greedy_safety: REASSOCIATING -> breaks byte-exactness on 3/4 shapes (qkv, o_proj, down; gate_up stays bit-exact) — stark #448
knob_A_in_tree_python_proxy.lives_in: the version-pinned served vLLM wheel: vllm-0.22.1rc1.dev307+g3e8afdf78 (manifest.dependencies[0], version-pinned)
knob_A_in_tree_python_proxy.role: SUB-PRIZE PROXY (proves the FP-reassociation hazard; NOT the prize)
knob_A_in_tree_python_proxy.why_not_the_prize: only +0.64 TPS UB (sub-bar). The ~16% BW prize is NOT cashed by the accumulation-dtype flag alone; it needs the K-reduction re-partition below (knob B).

knob_B_build_level_split_k_repartition.build_artifact: a PATCHED vLLM Marlin wheel (or a custom kernel shipped in the submission) realizing the re-partitioned K-reduction
knob_B_build_level_split_k_repartition.current_value: deployed in-kernel split-K geometry = f(M) auto-selection (pinned to the deployed reduction order -> identity 0.9966, 3 flips)
knob_B_build_level_split_k_repartition.kernel_source: Marlin int4 W4A16 CUDA kernel (csrc/quantization/gptq_marlin/*) inside the served vLLM wheel
knob_B_build_level_split_k_repartition.role: THE PRIZE (+17.05 realistic / +29.34 ceiling) — re-partitions the K-reduction
knob_B_build_level_split_k_repartition.symbol: split-K geometry (num_splits / max_par), BLOCK_K tile, num_warps — AUTO-SELECTED in-kernel as f(M); NO exposed Python knob (stark #448 / kanna #122)

prize_location.bw_slack: ~16% achieved-BW slack on the int4-Marlin GEMM (#450 roofline)
prize_location.provenance: ubel #450 c5oyb7gv roofline + stark #448 fn4iz0dz knob audit

served_change_spec.summary: PRIZE = the int4-Marlin W4A16 K-reduction split-K re-partition (+17.05 realistic / +29.34 ceiling over deployed 481.53). NO Python knob — needs a kernel BUILD (patched Marlin wheel). The in-tree `use_fp32_reduce=True->False` flag is only a +0.64 sub-prize proxy. Every realized geometry value is a <PENDING #452> slot; applying is human-gated (Directive #3).

validation_harness_spec.all_pass_means: SHIP-READY (== recommend() GO on a valid 128/128 run)
validation_harness_spec.any_fail_means: HOLD (CI-AMBIGUOUS gain) or ROLLBACK (hard fail) — see (3)/(4)

clauses.check: measured relax TPS >= deployed 481.53 + human bar B, CI-CLEAN at 1 sigma_hw (gain - 4.8153 >= B)
clauses.decision_surface_anchor: decision_flip_tps_threshold=12.2346 (largest CI-clean bar for the realistic +17.05; #462)

clauses.check: measured relax PPL <= 2.42 (HARD gate; #462 most-sensitive input, margin 0.0428 from anchor 2.3772)
clauses.recommend_clause: clause-2: measured_ppl > PPL_GATE -> NO-GO  (hard quality fail)

clauses.check: flip-KIND is same-family accumulation-order near-ties (the deployed reduction-order family), NOT a new quality-destroying mode
clauses.decision_surface_anchor: #462 clause-3 / N-invariance: the count N is orthogonal; only the categorical KIND gates
clauses.recommend_clause: clause-3: not break_same_kind -> NO-GO  (new failure mode)
clauses.stark452_metric: measured_relax_flip_kind  (the KIND, NEVER the count N — orthogonal)

clauses.recommend_clause: recommend() DOMAIN PRECONDITION: gain/ppl are only defined on a valid 128/128 run; an incomplete run is invalid -> ROLLBACK (never reaches GO)

validation_harness_spec.summary: 4-clause checklist: (a) TPS CI-clean >= deployed+B at 1 sigma_hw -> recommend clause-1; (b) PPL <= 2.42 -> clause-2; (c) same-KIND flips -> clause-3; (d) 128/128 -> recommend domain precondition. Every check maps to a #462 recommend() clause.
